critical_issues: S3 loss explosion: FEM fine-tuning losses in hundreds of millions, indicating gradient/scale instability
Model learned shortcut: outputs near-zero phi for simple geometries, completely fails on complex ones (cantilever, tower)
S1 warm-up target is non-physical (tiled E/nu/occ), giving no useful structural prior
S2 final step exploded to 8.3B, likely due to mixup with corrupted targets or hypernet-style instability (not applicable here)
diagnosis.hypothesis: The combination of meaningless S1 targets + unbalanced multi-task loss in S3 (physics_residual + hybrid_task) causes the model to collapse into a trivial solution. FEM stress scale (3.7e5) may also create massive gradients when combined with physics residual.

next_action: Remove physics_residual_loss from S3 (proven unstable in batched mode)
Replace S1 with meaningful spectral-alignment or simple LEA-based warm-up
Increase S3 batch_size to 4 and steps to 300+ for stable FEM learning
Add NaN/Inf detection and per-task loss logging
Consider removing mixup from S3 (FEM dataclass cannot be mixed)
